content:
Case 9:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 81
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: New-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with localized pain and reduced range of motion
- Vital signs: BP 118/70 mmHg, HR 92/min (irregular), Temp 36.9 °C, RR 18/min, SpO₂ 95 % (room air)
Setting: Outpatient clinic (initial presentation with localized musculoskeletal swelling)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in an ambulatory hematology setting. You can obtain a detailed history and examination, request routine blood work, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging appropriate for an initial assessment. Invasive biopsy procedures and complex diagnostic evaluations require additional planning.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Obtain routine blood work including complete blood count (CBC), coagulation studies (PT, INR, aPTT), basic metabolic panel, and inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR)."}}

response.request: Obtain routine blood work including complete blood count (CBC), coagulation studies (PT, INR, aPTT), basic metabolic panel, and inflammatory markers (CRP, ESR).

content:
Cbc Coagulation:
CBC: Hb 10.4 g/dL, WBC 6.1 × 10⁹/L, Plt 104 × 10⁹/L; MCV 90 fL, MCH 29 pg. Differential: neutrophils 60 %, lymphocytes 28 %, monocytes 8 %, eosinophils 3 %, basophils 1 %.
Coagulation: aPTT 31 s, INR 1.6.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Perform ultrasound of the right shoulder and upper arm to evaluate for hematoma, abscess, or other soft tissue pathology."}}

response.request: Perform ultrasound of the right shoulder and upper arm to evaluate for hematoma, abscess, or other soft tissue pathology.

content:
'Perform ultrasound of the right shoulder and upper arm to evaluate for hematoma, abscess, or other soft tissue pathology.' not available. Request a specific test or report.
This case does not include a right-shoulder or extremity soft-tissue ultrasound. You could request related imaging that is available (e.g., CT skeleton) or review the physical exam (history_physical) to assess for local swelling or signs of infection.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Provide detailed history and physical examination findings (history_physical), including assessment of the localized swelling, signs of infection, bruising, or other relevant details."}}

response.request: Provide detailed history and physical examination findings (history_physical), including assessment of the localized swelling, signs of infection, bruising, or other relevant details.

content:
History Physical:
2025-07-03: The patient presented to the hematology outpatient clinic with new-onset swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm, which developed over the past several weeks and was associated with localized pain and reduced range of motion. He also reported foamy urine, but denied constitutional B symptoms such as fever, night sweats, or unintentional weight loss. There was no recent history of infection or trauma. Physical examination revealed asymmetric swelling of the right shoulder and upper arm with mild tenderness to palpation. No overlying erythema or warmth was noted. No palpable cervical, axillary, or inguinal lymphadenopathy. Lungs were clear on auscultation; cardiac rhythm was irregularly irregular. The abdomen was soft and non-tender without hepatosplenomegaly. No peripheral edema was present. Neurological exam showed intact cranial nerves and normal gait.
Current medication: allopurinol 100 mg 1-0-0, calcium carbonate 500 mg 1-0-0, cotrimoxazole 480 mg 1-0-0 (Mon/Wed/Fri), tamsulosin 0.4 mg 0-0-1, ramipril 5 mg 1-0-0, torasemide 10 mg 0-1-0, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-0, ophthalmic timolol 0.5% (1 drop both eyes BID), atorvastatin 10 mg 0-0-1, apixaban 2.5 mg 1-0-1, bisoprolol 2.5 mg 1-0-1, and pregabalin 75 mg 1-0-1.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Obtain basic metabolic panel (BMP including creatinine and albumin), urinalysis with protein quantification, serum protein electrophoresis (SPEP) with immunofixation, and free light chain assay."}}

response.request: Obtain basic metabolic panel (BMP including creatinine and albumin), urinalysis with protein quantification, serum protein electrophoresis (SPEP) with immunofixation, and free light chain assay.

content:
'protein_studies' available in round 2.
The protein studies (urine studies, SPEP/IFE, and free light chains) are part of the protein_studies item, but that item is not available in round 1. You can request chemistry_immunology now to obtain the BMP/renal function and serum albumin; consider requesting protein_studies in the next round for SPEP/IFE, urine protein quantification, and free light chains.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Obtain chemistry_immunology results, including basic metabolic panel (BMP), creatinine, albumin, and any relevant immunology markers."}}

response.request: Obtain chemistry_immunology results, including basic metabolic panel (BMP), creatinine, albumin, and any relevant immunology markers.

content:
Chemistry Immunology:
Chemistry: Na 134 mmol/L, K 5.1 mmol/L, creatinine 2.3 mg/dL (eGFR 26 mL/min/1.73 m²), BUN 48 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL, LDH 265 U/L, uric acid 8.2 mg/dL, Ca²⁺ 3.02 mmol/L, phosphate 4.9 mg/dL.
Immunology: IgG 1820 mg/dL, free κ light chains 412 mg/L, κ/λ ratio 48.
Urinalysis: protein +++ without hematuria.
NT-proBNP 2350 pg/mL.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Multiple myeloma", "differentials": ["AL amyloidosis", "Light chain deposition disease", "Waldenstrom macroglobulinemia", "Metastatic bone disease"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Urgent referral for bone marrow biopsy and skeletal survey (or whole-body low-dose CT)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate bisphosphonate therapy (e.g., zoledronic acid) for hypercalcemia and myeloma bone disease", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "IV hydration and consider loop diuretics if volume overload, to address renal failure", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Hold or adjust apixaban dose given renal impairment and supratherapeutic INR; bridge if necessary", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Order serum protein electrophoresis (SPEP) with immunofixation and 24-hour urine protein electrophoresis if not already included", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}]}}}

treatment_plan.action: Urgent referral for bone marrow biopsy and skeletal survey (or whole-body low-dose CT)
Initiate bisphosphonate therapy (e.g., zoledronic acid) for hypercalcemia and myeloma bone disease
IV hydration and consider loop diuretics if volume overload, to address renal failure
Hold or adjust apixaban dose given renal impairment and supratherapeutic INR; bridge if necessary
Order serum protein electrophoresis (SPEP) with immunofixation and 24-hour urine protein electrophoresis if not already included

You can organize bone marrow and tissue sampling, detailed histopathology and immunophenotyping, comprehensive staging imaging, and extended laboratory evaluations. Interdisciplinary consultations are available. Some specialized test results may still be pending.
Request additional information or solve.

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Obtain protein_studies including serum protein electrophoresis (SPEP) with immunofixation, urine protein electrophoresis (UPEP) with immunofixation, and 24-hour urine protein quantification."}}

response.request: Obtain protein_studies including serum protein electrophoresis (SPEP) with immunofixation, urine protein electrophoresis (UPEP) with immunofixation, and 24-hour urine protein quantification.

content:
Protein Studies:
Urinalysis: proteinuria (++), leukocyturia (120/µL), positive for albumin; κ/λ free light-chain ratio 18.3.
Serum protein electrophoresis revealed a distinct monoclonal IgG κ band with an M-spike, confirmed by immunofixation. Urine electrophoresis demonstrated monoclonal κ light-chain proteinuria.
β₂-microglobulin 4.8 mg/L, serum albumin 3.2 g/dL.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Symptomatic multiple myeloma (IgG kappa type) with CRAB features (hypercalcemia, renal failure, anemia, bone/soft tissue involvement)", "differentials": ["Metastatic bone disease", "Extramedullary plasmacytoma", "Light chain deposition disease"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, FISH for myeloma risk stratification", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Whole-body low-dose CT or skeletal survey to assess for lytic lesions", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "MRI of right shoulder and upper arm to evaluate for pathological fracture, plasmacytoma, or hematoma", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Administer IV bisphosphonate (zoledronic acid 4 mg) for hypercalcemia and bone disease prevention", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "High-volume IV isotonic saline hydration (target 2-3 L/day) with close monitoring of volume status and electrolytes for renal support", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Nephrology consultation for evaluation of light chain cast nephropathy; consider extracorporeal light chain removal if indicated", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Review and adjust anticoagulation (apixaban) in context of thrombocytopenia, renal impairment, and possible hematoma; consider bridging with heparin if high thrombotic risk", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Symptomatic multiple myeloma (IgG kappa type) with CRAB features (hypercalcemia, renal failure, anemia, bone/soft tissue involvement)

treatment_plan.action: Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, FISH for myeloma risk stratification
Whole-body low-dose CT or skeletal survey to assess for lytic lesions
MRI of right shoulder and upper arm to evaluate for pathological fracture, plasmacytoma, or hematoma
Administer IV bisphosphonate (zoledronic acid 4 mg) for hypercalcemia and bone disease prevention
High-volume IV isotonic saline hydration (target 2-3 L/day) with close monitoring of volume status and electrolytes for renal support
Nephrology consultation for evaluation of light chain cast nephropathy; consider extracorporeal light chain removal if indicated
Review and adjust anticoagulation (apixaban) in context of thrombocytopenia, renal impairment, and possible hematoma; consider bridging with heparin if high thrombotic risk

Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Provide bone marrow aspiration and biopsy results, including morphology, flow cytometry, cytogenetics, FISH panel for myeloma (including t(4;14), t(14;16), del(17p), etc.), and plasma cell percentage."}}

response.request: Provide bone marrow aspiration and biopsy results, including morphology, flow cytometry, cytogenetics, FISH panel for myeloma (including t(4;14), t(14;16), del(17p), etc.), and plasma cell percentage.

content:
Bone Marrow:
Bone marrow smears were normocellular with approximately 5-10 % plasma cells. The plasma cells appeared large with eccentric nuclei, coarse “clock-face” chromatin, prominent nucleoli, and abundant basophilic cytoplasm. Occasional binucleated forms were noted. The background marrow showed mildly reduced granulopoiesis and dysmorphic erythropoiesis, with scattered small lymphocytes and occasional mast cells. No excess of blasts was identified. Flow cytometry revealed 0.9 % plasma cells with strong expression of CD38⁺, CD138⁺, and CD27⁺ and dim CD56 expression, showing cytoplasmic kappa light-chain restriction. CD19 and CD45 were negative. No aberrant myeloid or B-cell populations were detected, 1.2% CD34low blasts.
Histology (core biopsy) demonstrated age-adjusted normocellular marrow (overall cellularity 40 %) with nodular and interstitial plasma-cell infiltrates, accounting for 5-10 % of total marrow cellularity. Immunohistochemistry confirmed CD138⁺ plasma cells with kappa light-chain restriction, negative for lambda. Residual trilineage hematopoiesis was present but mildly reduced with dysplastic erythropoiesis.

{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Provide cytogenetics, FISH results (including t(4;14), t(14;16), del(17p), gain(1q), etc.), full staging imaging (whole-body low-dose CT or skeletal survey, MRI or CT of right shoulder and upper arm), and any additional pathology such as shoulder biopsy if performed."}}

response.request: Provide cytogenetics, FISH results (including t(4;14), t(14;16), del(17p), gain(1q), etc.), full staging imaging (whole-body low-dose CT or skeletal survey, MRI or CT of right shoulder and upper arm), and any additional pathology such as shoulder biopsy if performed.

content:
Cytogenetics Fish:
FISH analysis revealed trisomy 3, t(4;14)(p16;q32) involving IGH::FGFR3/MMSET, deletion 13q14, deletion of CDKN2A, gain of 1q21, and deletion of 17p.
Conventional cytogenetics demonstrated a complex karyotype in the CD138-negative fraction.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "High-risk IgG kappa multiple myeloma (R-ISS stage III) with CRAB features (hypercalcemia, renal impairment, anemia, bone/extramedullary involvement as right shoulder plasmacytoma) and high-risk cytogenetics (t(4;14), del(17p), gain(1q))", "differentials": ["Extramedullary plasmacytoma with systemic involvement", "AL amyloidosis", "Metastatic solid tumor to bone/soft tissue"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate urgent treatment with daratumumab-bortezomib-melphalan-prednisone (Dara-VMP) or daratumumab-lenalidomide-dexamethasone (Dara-Rd) adjusted for renal function and frailty; consider clinical trial enrollment for high-risk disease", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "IV bisphosphonates (zoledronate 4 mg adjusted for CrCl or denosumab 120 mg SC) monthly for hypercalcemia and bone disease prevention", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Aggressive IV hydration (normal saline 1-2 L/day adjusted for cardiac status) \u00b1 high-dose dexamethasone for myeloma cast nephropathy; urgent nephrology consult for plasmapheresis or high-cutoff dialysis if light chain >2000 mg/L or rapid progression", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Whole-body low-dose CT and/or PET-CT for full staging; MRI right shoulder to characterize extramedullary mass/plasmacytoma \u00b1 biopsy if feasible", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Hold apixaban due to thrombocytopenia, renal failure, supratherapeutic INR, and possible retroperitoneal/shoulder hematoma; cardiology consult for bridging with LMWH if CHA2DS2-VASc high", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Prophylaxis: acyclovir/valganciclovir continued, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole for PCP, atorvastatin continue, allopurinol continue; monitor for bortezomib neuropathy (already on pregabalin)", "intention": "supportive", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Geriatric/frailty assessment (IMWG score) and comorbidity index (Charlson) to guide intensity; palliative care involvement for symptom management (shoulder pain, mobility)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: High-risk IgG kappa multiple myeloma (R-ISS stage III) with CRAB features (hypercalcemia, renal impairment, anemia, bone/extramedullary involvement as right shoulder plasmacytoma) and high-risk cytogenetics (t(4;14), del(17p), gain(1q))
differentials: Metastatic solid tumor to bone/soft tissue

treatment_plan.action: Initiate urgent treatment with daratumumab-bortezomib-melphalan-prednisone (Dara-VMP) or daratumumab-lenalidomide-dexamethasone (Dara-Rd) adjusted for renal function and frailty; consider clinical trial enrollment for high-risk disease
IV bisphosphonates (zoledronate 4 mg adjusted for CrCl or denosumab 120 mg SC) monthly for hypercalcemia and bone disease prevention

Aggressive IV hydration (normal saline 1-2 L/day adjusted for cardiac status) ± high-dose dexamethasone for myeloma cast nephropathy; urgent nephrology consult for plasmapheresis or high-cutoff dialysis if light chain >2000 mg/L or rapid progression
Whole-body low-dose CT and/or PET-CT for full staging; MRI right shoulder to characterize extramedullary mass/plasmacytoma ± biopsy if feasible
Hold apixaban due to thrombocytopenia, renal failure, supratherapeutic INR, and possible retroperitoneal/shoulder hematoma; cardiology consult for bridging with LMWH if CHA2DS2-VASc high
Prophylaxis: acyclovir/valganciclovir continued, trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole for PCP, atorvastatin continue, allopurinol continue; monitor for bortezomib neuropathy (already on pregabalin)
Geriatric/frailty assessment (IMWG score) and comorbidity index (Charlson) to guide intensity; palliative care involvement for symptom management (shoulder pain, mobility)
